title: Garth Breise
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Garth}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Garth Breise |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'', which includes the appearance of Garth Breise and [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Death/Legends|death]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]], to [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Extragalactic Society]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Way to go, newbie.|Garth Breise, to Yomin Carr|}} '''Garth Breise''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Astronomer/Legends|astronomer]] at the scientific organization known as the [[Extragalactic Society]]. He was stationed at [[ExGal-4]] [[Listening post/Legends|exploratory base]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]], where he worked the [[Standard day/Legends|night]] shift at the facility's [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] station by [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. Breise also filled the position of [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] at the base and was often tasked with technical assignments, such as building proprietary equipment. Well-liked at ExGal-4, Breise regularly conversed and played the [[Board game/Legends|board game]] [[Dejarik/Legends|dejarik]] with his colleagues. He also worked alongside [[Yuuzhan Vong]] advance agent [[Yomin Carr]], who was [[Ooglith masquer|incognito]] at ExGal-4 to prevent any transmissions from alerting [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|imminent threat]] that the Yuuzhan Vong posed. During an inspection of the base's communications tower, which Carr had [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]], Breise was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the Yuuzhan Vong agent to deprive the base of its engineer and effectively sever its link to the outside. ==Biography== ===Career at ExGal=== {{Quote|He'll get bored with it soon enough.|Garth Breise, discussing Yomin Carr's enthusiasm for working at ExGal-4|audio=GarthBreise2.flac|[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime'' abridged audiobook]]}} [[File:ExGal4map_RPG.jpg|thumb|right|200px|ExGal-4 on Belkadan, where Garth Breise worked]] A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Astronomer/Legends|astronomer]] employed by the scientific organization known as the [[Extragalactic Society]], Breise worked at the [[ExGal-4]] [[Listening post/Legends|listening post]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. At some point between [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]] and [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events on Belkadan as depicted in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' to [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]; ''Vector Prime'' states that [[Danni Quee]] had been with the establishing crew of [[ExGal-4]], and had been there three [[Standard year/Legends|years]], thus meaning Breise must have come to work there at some point between [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]] and [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. he became part of the team monitoring the edges of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for signs of [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] life, working [[Standard day/Legends|night]] shift at the base. He was initially elated at being assigned to ExGal-4 and the possibility of being part of any extra-galactic discoveries, a trait which the team generally attributed to "[[Noob|newbie]]" excitement. He came to find the posting monotonous, remarking that any new recruits would become fed up eventually. His primary responsibility was manning his [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] station while observing and monitoring areas of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] for a scientific anomaly or discovery that would benefit the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]; in addition, he also had [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] acumen. ===A dark discovery=== {{Quote|Two days.''"
"''Make it tomorrow. We want to give them time to get on the spot.|Garth Breise and Danni Quee argue over the repairs to ExGal-4's communications|}} In 25 ABY, ExGal member [[Danni Quee]] detected an [[Koros-Strohna|unusual object]] entering the [[Helska system/Legends|Helska system]]; Breise calculated the that trajectory of it would impact the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] [[Helska IV|fourth planet]]. Amid a heated debate among the team over how to proceed with this information, rookie [[Yomin Carr]] proposed that a detachment investigate and report their findings to ExGal proper. A [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]]-intoxicated Breise was quick to praise Carr for settling the argument, unaware that his colleague's Human appearance was only a [[Ooglith masquer|disguise]]; Carr was secretly an advance agent for the [[Praetorite Vong]], a vanguard force for the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]], and had been aware of the importance of that [[Vector Prime|point]] in space. A team that included Breise's good friend [[Bensin Tomri]] was scrambled to investigate the anomaly; however, the base could not make contact with any outside agency due to damage to ExGal-4's [[Communications array/Legends|communications tower]], which was [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] secretly perpetrated by Carr to protect preparations for his people's [[Yuuzhan Vong War|impending invasion]]. Breise would stay behind to repair the fault, a task that he felt obligated to perform regardless of his disdain for it—being reprimanded by his colleague Quee was the outcome Breise wished to avoid. When Breise and Carr went to climb and repair the damaged communications tower, Breise happened upon several small [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Beetle/Legends|beetles]] on the ground around the tower. He was unaware that they were [[Biot|Yuuzhan Vong-developed creatures]] known as [[dweebit]]s that Carr himself had introduced into Belkadan's ecology to aid in his people's future [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] efforts there. Barely noticing the [[gas]] that the dweebits were producing and reluctant to blame any of the tower's damage on the beetles as Carr suggested, Breise decided to see to any problems at the top of the tower first, and he led the way up the construct. ===The last task=== {{Quote|Why?|Garth Breise, shortly before being killed by Yomin Carr|}} [[File:Yomin_Carr.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Yomin Carr, Garth Breise's killer]] Knowing that any transmissions resulting from a successful repairing of the tower would likely call attention to the incursion of the Praetorite Vong, Carr made ready to ambush Breise at the summit of the tower. While Breise was momentarily distracted by the departure of the base's [[Spacecaster-class shuttle|Spacecaster]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], which carried the outbound team members, Carr struck Breise's windpipe. As Breise put one [[Limb/Legends|hand]] to his [[Throat/Legends|throat]], Carr struck him again on the wrist of the hand that was holding on to the tower before cutting Breise's climbing [[Rope/Legends|rope]] with a [[coufee]] [[Dagger/Legends|blade]]. Despite his efforts to regain his balance, Breise fell a hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] to his [[Death/Legends|death]], hitting the side of the tower and finally landing on the ground. Carr momentarily expressed regret—not for Breise's killing, but for the probability that his falling body had crushed several dweebits on the ground. Following Breise's death, the other present members of the ExGal-4 team were immensely upset. Tomri never learned of Breise's fate, as he died on Helska IV. [[Tee-ubo Doole]], another team member, dreaded informing Tomri of Breise's death, but she remained unaware of Tomri's fate before dying from the gas unleashed by Carr's dweebits, which had reached toxic levels. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I just want to get this stupid work over with.|Garth Breise, despairing of his latest task|}} Breise was an active member of ExGal-4, both in professional and social respects. Competent and hardworking despite apathy towards his job, he was also an avid conversationalist, and discussing new members at the base was one of his favorite subjects. Moreover, he praised intelligent observations and discoveries made by his colleagues. Breise also enjoyed playing the [[Board game/Legends|board game]] [[Dejarik/Legends|dejarik]] with other team members, such as Tomri, Doole, and Quee, and partook in the recreational use of ryll [[Spice/Legends|spice]] alongside Tomri. While Breise's duties mainly revolved around astronomy, which he eventually found to be boring and pointed out that new member Carr would come to find boring as well, his engineering skills meant that repair jobs were handed over to him, such as building a connector to sort cables after the ExGal-4 communications tower was damaged. Despite his dislike of certain responsibilities at ExGal-4, he took them on with aplomb, as refusal meant being berated by Quee. Even so, Breise did not hesitate to express sullenness and sarcasm if he felt put upon. While going to effect repairs on the base's communications tower, Breise was safety-conscious, acting on concerns raised by Carr and using ExGal-4's [[Floodlight/Legends|floodlights]] to check for any potential dangers near the tower—namely, Belkadan's [[redcrested cougar]]s. ==Equipment== Being an engineer, Breise had access to the tools necessary for building a connector for sorting various cables for ExGal-4's communications tower, and carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on his [[Belt/Legends|belt]] when personally investigating the outage at the construct alongside Carr. ==Behind the scenes== Breise was introduced in the [[1999]] novel [[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime|''Vector Prime'']], the first installment of the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series, by [[R. A. Salvatore]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' {{1st|[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|audiobook]]}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Garth Breise}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Garth Breise |es=Garth Breise |pl=Garth Breise }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Breise, Garth}} [[Category:Astronomers]] [[Category:Engineers]] [[Category:Extragalactic Society members]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]]
